facts.text: Kraftwerk, formed in Düsseldorf in 1970, is widely regarded as the most influential group in electronic music history.
The Roland TR-808 drum machine, released in 1980, became one of the most iconic instruments in electronic music despite initially being a commercial failure.
The term 'techno' was coined in Detroit in the mid-1980s by Juan Atkins, Derrick May, and Kevin Saunderson, known as the Belleville Three.
The Moog synthesizer, invented by Robert Moog in 1964, was one of the first widely used electronic instruments.
House music originated in Chicago in the early 1980s, named after the Warehouse nightclub where DJ Frankie Knuckles played.
Tomorrowland, held annually in Boom, Belgium, is one of the largest electronic music festivals in the world, attracting over 400,000 attendees.
Avicii's real name was Tim Bergling, and he was from Stockholm, Sweden.
The Roland TB-303 Bass Line synthesizer, originally designed to accompany solo guitarists, became the defining sound of acid house music.
The first commercial synthesizer, the RCA Mark II, was installed at the Columbia-Princeton Electronic Music Center in 1957.
Daft Punk's 'Around the World' single repeats the phrase 'around the world' 144 times throughout the song.
The Haçienda nightclub in Manchester, opened by Factory Records and New Order in 1982, was a pivotal venue for electronic music in the UK.
Ibiza became a global hub for electronic music in the late 1980s when British DJs began hosting club nights on the island.
The Love Parade, an electronic music festival held in Berlin from 1989 to 2010, once attracted over 1.5 million attendees.
Aphex Twin's album 'Selected Ambient Works 85–92' is considered one of the greatest electronic albums ever made.
Deadmau5's iconic mouse helmet was originally a homemade creation he wore as a joke at a Halloween party.
The term EDM, standing for Electronic Dance Music, became widely used in North America around 2010 to describe the growing mainstream genre.
The Minimoog, released in 1970, was the first portable synthesizer and made electronic music accessible to touring musicians.
The rave scene in the UK during the late 1980s led to the Criminal Justice and Public Order Act 1994, which restricted outdoor gatherings with repetitive beats.
David Guetta has sold over 50 million records worldwide and is one of the best-selling French artists of all time.
Calvin Harris became the first artist to have nine singles from one album reach the top 10 on the Billboard Hot 100.
Skrillex won three Grammy Awards in 2012, including Best New Artist at the 54th Grammy Awards.
The Yamaha DX7, released in 1983, was the first commercially successful digital synthesizer, selling over 200,000 units.
Drum and bass originated in the early 1990s in England, evolving from the breakbeat hardcore and jungle scenes.
The Roland TR-909, released in 1983, provided the distinctive hi-hat and kick drum sounds that defined house and techno music.
Ultra Music Festival in Miami began in 1999 as a one-day event on the sands of Miami Beach.
Marshmello keeps his identity hidden behind a custom marshmallow-shaped helmet during all public appearances.
The genre of dubstep originated in South London in the late 1990s, characterized by heavy bass and syncopated rhythms.
Kraftwerk's 1974 album 'Autobahn' featured a 22-minute title track that became an international hit.
The Electric Daisy Carnival (EDC) in Las Vegas is the largest electronic music festival in North America, regularly attracting over 400,000 people.
The Korg MS-20, released in 1978, remains one of the most popular analog synthesizers among electronic musicians.
Giorgio Moroder's 1977 album 'From Here to Eternity' is considered one of the first fully electronic disco albums.
Frankie Knuckles, known as the Godfather of House Music, began DJing at the Warehouse club in Chicago in 1977.
Ableton Live, released in 2001, revolutionized electronic music production by enabling both studio production and live performance.
The BBC Radiophonic Workshop, established in 1958, pioneered electronic sound design, most famously creating the Doctor Who theme.
Trance music emerged in the early 1990s in Germany, characterized by repetitive melodic phrases and a musical form that builds and releases tension.
Brian Eno is credited with coining the term 'ambient music' with his 1978 album 'Ambient 1: Music for Airports'.
The first electronic music concert was held by Karlheinz Stockhausen in Cologne in 1953, using tape recorders and oscillators.
FL Studio, originally called FruityLoops, was first released in 1997 and became one of the most popular DAWs for EDM production.
Hardstyle originated in the Netherlands around 2000, combining hard trance, hard house, and hardcore elements.
The term 'IDM' (Intelligent Dance Music) was coined in the early 1990s to describe experimental electronic music by artists like Aphex Twin and Autechre.
Skrillex's real name is Sonny John Moore, and he was previously the lead vocalist of the post-hardcore band From First to Last.
Tiësto performed at the opening ceremony of the 2004 Athens Olympics, making him the first DJ to play at an Olympic Games.
Pioneer's CDJ series, first released in 1994, became the industry standard for DJing and replaced vinyl turntables in most clubs.
Jean-Michel Jarre's 1976 album 'Oxygène' sold over 18 million copies and popularized electronic music globally.
Deadmau5's real name is Joel Thomas Zimmerman, and he is from Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada.
The first major commercial sampler, the Fairlight CMI, was released in 1979 and cost around $25,000.
Daft Punk consisted of two members, Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, who met in Paris in 1987.
Synthwave, also known as retrowave, emerged in the mid-2000s as a genre inspired by 1980s film soundtracks and video game music.
The Roland Juno-106, released in 1984, became one of the most widely used synthesizers in electronic and pop music.
Berghain, a nightclub in Berlin housed in a former power plant, is considered one of the most famous techno clubs in the world.
Avicii's song 'Wake Me Up' became the most streamed song on Spotify at the time of its release in 2013.
Marshmello's 2017 Fortnite concert was watched by over 10 million players simultaneously in-game.
Richie Hawtin, also known as Plastikman, is credited with pushing the boundaries of minimal techno in the 1990s.
The Buchla Music Box, created by Don Buchla in 1963, was one of the first modular synthesizers.
The Chemical Brothers won six Grammy Awards and are considered pioneers of big beat electronic music.
Detroit's Music Institute, opened by Derrick May in 1988, was the first club dedicated exclusively to techno music.
The ARP 2600, first manufactured in 1971, was used to create the voice of R2-D2 in Star Wars.
Armin van Buuren has hosted over 1,000 episodes of his radio show 'A State of Trance,' which reaches millions of listeners worldwide.
The Akai MPC, first released in 1988, revolutionized sampling and beat-making in both hip-hop and electronic music.
Burial's self-titled debut album in 2006 helped establish the UK dubstep scene and was released on the Hyperdub label.
The warehouse rave scene in Detroit and Chicago in the 1980s laid the groundwork for the global EDM movement.
Calvin Harris's real name is Adam Richard Wiles, and he is from Dumfries, Scotland.
Paul Oakenfold brought the Ibiza sound to the UK after visiting the island in 1987, helping spark the acid house movement.
Skrillex founded the record label OWSLA in 2011, which has signed artists like Zedd, Flume, and Porter Robinson.
The Paradise Garage nightclub in New York, where Larry Levan was resident DJ from 1977 to 1987, helped define the garage house genre.
Native Instruments released Traktor DJ software in 2000, transforming digital DJing from a novelty into a professional tool.
The concept of beat matching, essential to modern DJing, was pioneered by Francis Grasso in New York in the early 1970s.
Fatboy Slim's real name is Norman Cook, and he previously played bass in the indie band The Housemartins.
The genre of electro emerged in the early 1980s, fusing funk rhythms with electronic production, pioneered by Afrika Bambaataa.
Daft Punk's 2001 album 'Discovery' was accompanied by an anime film called 'Interstella 5555,' directed by Leiji Matsumoto.
The Oberheim OB-Xa, released in 1980, was featured prominently in Van Halen's 'Jump' and many early electronic tracks.
The MIDI protocol, introduced in 1983, allowed electronic instruments from different manufacturers to communicate with each other.
Amnesia in Ibiza opened in 1976 and became one of the island's most legendary electronic music venues.
The first modern DJ mixer was created by Rudy Bozak in the 1960s and became an industry standard for decades.
The genre of progressive house was popularized in the early 1990s by DJs like Sasha and John Digweed.
Moby's album 'Play,' released in 1999, had every track licensed for commercial use, making it one of the most commercially successful electronic albums.
Kraftwerk performed their live shows with custom-built robotic mannequins of themselves on stage.
The Sequential Circuits Prophet-5, released in 1978, was the first polyphonic synthesizer with programmable memory.
The Berlin Wall's fall in 1989 led to the explosion of techno culture in reunified Berlin, with abandoned buildings becoming legendary clubs.
The Access Virus synthesizer, popular in trance production, was first released in 1997 by the German company Kemper.
Carl Cox has been a key figure in global techno and house music for over three decades, known for his marathon DJ sets.
The theremin, invented in 1920 by Léon Theremin, is one of the earliest electronic instruments and is played without physical contact.
The first commercially available vocoder was the Sennheiser VSM 201 in 1977, which became a staple in electronic music.
Massive Attack's 1991 album 'Blue Lines' is often credited as the first trip-hop album, blending electronic production with hip-hop and soul.
Illenium blends melodic bass with emotional songwriting and has become one of the most popular artists in the future bass genre.
The genre of gabber, an extremely fast and aggressive form of electronic music, originated in Rotterdam in the early 1990s.
The 303 acid sound was discovered by accident when DJ Pierre of Phuture experimented with a TB-303 in Chicago in 1985.
Ministry of Sound, a nightclub in London established in 1991, became one of the most famous dance music brands globally.
The Korg Volca series, introduced in 2013, brought affordable analog synthesis to bedroom producers.
Paul van Dyk was one of the first trance DJs to receive a Grammy nomination.
The concept of the remix originated in Jamaican dub music but became a fundamental part of electronic music culture.
Aphex Twin's real name is Richard David James, and he was born in Limerick, Ireland, but raised in Cornwall, England.
DJing evolved from using two turntables and a mixer to sophisticated digital setups involving controllers, laptops, and custom software.
The Dave Smith Instruments Prophet-6, released in 2015, was a modern recreation of the classic Prophet-5 sound.
Tomorrowland's elaborate stage designs, especially its main stage, are built from scratch each year and have become iconic in the festival world.
The genre of future bass emerged around 2012, blending elements of trap, synth-pop, and UK garage.
Deadmau5 once live-streamed himself building an entire track from scratch in his studio, giving fans a rare look at his production process.
The Eurorack modular synthesizer format, introduced by Doepfer in 1996, sparked a massive resurgence in modular synthesis.
Sven Väth has been one of Germany's most important techno DJs since the early 1990s and founded the Cocoon label.
Deadmau5 built a custom Ferrari 458 wrapped in a Nyan Cat design, which he named the Purrari.
The Second Summer of Love in 1988 was a cultural phenomenon in Britain centered around acid house music and rave culture.
Zedd won a Grammy in 2014 for Best Dance Recording for his song 'Clarity' featuring Foxes.
Logic Pro X, originally developed by the German company Emagic, was acquired by Apple in 2002 and is a popular DAW for EDM.
Goldie's 1995 album 'Timeless' was a landmark release in the drum and bass genre and featured the epic 21-minute track 'Timeless.'
The Waldorf Blofeld, released in 2007, is a digital wavetable synthesizer popular with electronic musicians for its complex sound design.
Space Ibiza, which operated from 1989 to 2016, won the DJ Mag award for World's Best Club multiple times.
The Electric Daisy Carnival moved from Los Angeles to Las Vegas in 2011 after safety concerns at the LA venue.
The Roland SP-404 sampler became essential in lo-fi and beat-making communities for its simplicity and portability.
The first laptop performance in electronic music is often attributed to artists at the end of the 1990s using software like Max/MSP.
Above & Beyond formed in 2000 and became one of the biggest trance acts in the world, known for their emotional Acoustic concerts.
Roger Sanchez won a Grammy in 2003 for Best Remixed Recording for his remix of No Doubt's 'Hella Good.'
Marshmello's real identity was widely speculated to be Chris Comstock, which was later confirmed by Forbes.
The Arturia MiniBrute, released in 2012, introduced many producers to affordable analog synthesis.
DJ Mag's Top 100 DJs poll, started in 1997, is the most widely followed popularity ranking of DJs in the world.
The genre of UK garage emerged in the mid-1990s in London, featuring syncopated rhythms and shuffled beats.
Flume won a Grammy for Best Dance/Electronic Album in 2017 for his album 'Skin.'
Underworld's 'Born Slippy .NUXX' became an iconic dance anthem after being featured in the 1996 film Trainspotting.
Eric Prydz's 'Call on Me' reached number one in multiple European countries and featured a controversial yet iconic music video.
The genre of breakbeat emerged in the late 1980s and is characterized by the use of sampled drum breaks.
Serato DJ, first released in 2004, became one of the most popular digital DJing platforms alongside Traktor.
The Mute Records label, founded by Daniel Miller in 1978, released music by Depeche Mode, Erasure, and other electronic artists.
Jeff Mills, known as 'The Wizard,' is a pioneer of Detroit techno famous for his rapid three-deck mixing technique.
Andy C is one of the most respected figures in drum and bass, known for his technical mixing and long DJ career.
The genre of industrial music, pioneered by Throbbing Gristle in the 1970s, blended electronic sounds with abrasive noise.
Boiler Room, an online music broadcasting platform launched in 2010, streams DJ sets and live performances from around the world.
The artform of turntablism emerged in the 1990s, with DJs using turntables as instruments through scratching and beat juggling.
The term 'brostep' was coined to describe the aggressive, mid-range-heavy style of dubstep popularized by Skrillex.
The Clavia Nord Lead, released in 1995, was the first virtual analog synthesizer and became popular in trance and pop music.
The Warp Records label, founded in Sheffield in 1989, released groundbreaking IDM music by Aphex Twin, Autechre, and LFO.
Alesso, born Alessandro Lindblad, is one of Sweden's most successful EDM exports.
The genre of psytrance originated in Goa, India, in the late 1980s, blending psychedelic rock influences with electronic beats.
Tiësto transitioned from trance to more mainstream EDM around 2009, broadening his appeal to festival audiences.
Knife Party was formed by Rob Swire and Gareth McGrillen of Pendulum as a side project focused on electro house and dubstep.
Daft Punk's 'Get Lucky' spent 15 weeks in the top five of the Billboard Hot 100 in 2013.
The Native Instruments Maschine, released in 2009, combined hardware pads with software sampling for beat production.
The Defqon.1 festival, organized by Q-dance, is one of the largest hardstyle festivals in the world, held in the Netherlands.
Diplo co-founded Major Lazer and is a member of the supergroup Jack Ü alongside Skrillex.
Autechre, consisting of Rob Brown and Sean Booth, have been releasing experimental electronic music since 1991 on Warp Records.
The genre of chillwave emerged around 2009, characterized by lo-fi production, synthesizers, and nostalgic aesthetics.
John Digweed's 'Northern Exposure' mix album series with Sasha helped define progressive house in the 1990s.
The Moog Grandmother, released in 2018, brought a semi-modular analog synthesizer to a new generation of musicians.
The genre of vaporwave, which emerged in the early 2010s, used slowed-down samples of 1980s and 1990s music as a form of cultural critique.
The genre of electro swing combines vintage swing and jazz samples with modern electronic production.
The Ableton Push controller, released in 2013, was designed specifically for music production workflow rather than DJing.
Larry Heard, known as Mr. Fingers, created 'Can You Feel It' in 1986, one of the earliest and most influential deep house tracks.
The Sonar festival in Barcelona has been running since 1994 and focuses on advanced electronic and multimedia art.
The Technics SL-1200 turntable, introduced in 1972, became the standard turntable for DJs worldwide for over 40 years.
Pendulum blended drum and bass with rock music, achieving mainstream success with albums like 'Hold Your Colour' in 2005.
The Teenage Engineering OP-1, released in 2011, is a portable synthesizer and sampler beloved for its unique design and workflow.
Tiësto's 'Adagio for Strings' is a trance reworking of Samuel Barber's orchestral composition from 1936.
SoundCloud, launched in 2007, became a crucial platform for emerging electronic music artists to share their work.
Bassnectar, whose real name is Lorin Ashton, was a prominent figure in the American bass music scene.
The genre of happy hardcore features fast tempos, typically 160–180 BPM, with euphoric melodies and pitched-up vocals.
Gesaffelstein, born Mike Lévy, is a French electronic musician known for his dark, industrial techno sound.
The Sequential Prophet-5 was the first synthesizer with the ability to save and recall patches.
The genre of lo-fi house emerged in the mid-2010s, embracing raw, unpolished production aesthetics reminiscent of early house music.
The term 'rave' originally referred to bohemian parties in 1950s London before being adopted by the electronic music scene in the 1980s.
The Elektron Digitakt, released in 2017, became a popular hardware sampler for live electronic performances.
Ricardo Villalobos is known for his minimal techno sets that often stretch beyond 10 hours.
The genre of bass house combines elements of house music with heavy bass drops and originated in the mid-2010s.
The Commodore 64 computer, released in 1982, had a SID sound chip that influenced early electronic music and the chiptune genre.
Aphex Twin released a track titled with a complex mathematical equation, and when visualized on a spectrograph, it revealed his face.
Sasha & John Digweed held a legendary residency at Twilo in New York from 1999 to 2001.
The genre of neurofunk is a subgenre of drum and bass characterized by complex, dark, and technical sound design.
Nero won a Grammy for Best Remixed Recording in 2013 for their remix of 'Promises.'
The Novation Bass Station, first released in 1993, was one of the first affordable analog bass synthesizers.
Sidechain compression, a technique where the bass ducks under the kick drum, became a hallmark of French house and EDM production.
The seminal compilation 'DJ-Kicks' series, started in 1996 on !K7 Records, has featured mixes from hundreds of electronic artists.
The genre of microhouse emerged in the early 2000s, minimizing traditional house elements to their most basic components.
Boys Noize, born Alexander Ridha, is a German DJ and producer who founded the Boysnoize Records label.
The Teenage Engineering OP-Z, released in 2018, can control lights, visuals, and Unity game engines alongside music production.
BT, born Brian Wayne Transeau, is known for pioneering the stutter edit technique in electronic music.
Laurent Garnier has been a central figure in French techno since the late 1980s and was one of the first French DJs to play in Manchester.
The PPG Wave synthesizer, built in Germany in the 1980s, pioneered wavetable synthesis technology.
Fisher, born Paul Nicholas Fisher, transitioned from professional surfing to house music production and DJing.
The genre of electroclash emerged in the late 1990s, blending electro, synthpop, and punk aesthetics.
The 'Superstar DJs' era of the late 1990s saw DJs like Paul Oakenfold and Fatboy Slim achieve rock-star levels of fame.
The Splice platform, launched in 2013, allows producers to rent-to-own plugins and access royalty-free samples.
Peggy Gou, a South Korean DJ based in Berlin, became one of the most prominent electronic artists in the late 2010s.
The genre of slow house or downtempo house typically plays at 100–115 BPM, slower than traditional house music.
The Roland SH-101, a monophonic synthesizer from 1982, became a staple of acid house and rave music.
Martin Garrix released 'Animals' when he was only 17 years old, and it reached number one in several countries.
Erol Alkan is a Turkish-British DJ who helped shape the indie-dance and electro scenes in London in the 2000s.
The genre of tech house blends elements of techno and house, typically featuring minimal arrangements with funky basslines.
The Sequential Circuits Pro-One, released in 1981, was a popular monophonic synthesizer for bass and lead sounds.
Green Velvet, born Curtis Alan Jones, is a Chicago DJ known for his eccentric persona and contribution to acid house.
The genre of witch house emerged around 2009, incorporating slowed-down hip-hop beats with dark, occult aesthetics.
The genre of dark ambient uses atmospheric, ominous soundscapes and was pioneered by artists like Lustmord in the 1980s.
The Korg Minilogue, released in 2016, brought four-voice polyphonic analog synthesis to a sub-$500 price point.
Nina Kraviz is a Russian DJ and producer known for her eclectic sets blending techno, acid, and industrial styles.
The Behringer TD-3, released in 2019, is an affordable clone of the Roland TB-303 that costs a fraction of the original.
The Warehouse nightclub in Chicago, where house music was born, was originally a members-only club catering primarily to the Black and gay community.
The genre of future funk emerged in the 2010s, combining French house production with Japanese city pop samples.
Ellen Allien founded the BPitch Control label in Berlin in 1999, releasing music by Modeselektor, Apparat, and others.
The CDJ-2000NXS2, released by Pioneer DJ in 2016, is considered the current gold standard for professional DJ equipment.
The genre of Eurodance dominated European charts in the 1990s with acts like La Bouche, Snap!, and 2 Unlimited.
Avicii's retirement from touring in 2016 brought attention to the mental and physical health challenges facing touring DJs.
Amelie Lens is a Belgian DJ who became one of the most in-demand techno artists in the late 2010s.
The Eventide H3000 effects processor, released in 1986, became legendary for its pitch-shifting and harmonizer effects in studios.
The first Ministry of Sound compilation album was released in 1993 and went on to become a best-selling series.
Deep house originated in Chicago in the mid-1980s, featuring jazzy, soulful elements and a more restrained sound than other house subgenres.
Orbital took their name from the M25 London Orbital motorway, where illegal raves were held in the late 1980s.
The Moog Mother-32, released in 2015, introduced many musicians to Eurorack-compatible semi-modular synthesis.
The genre of breakcore combines elements of jungle, drum and bass, and hardcore with complex, chaotic breakbeats.
Kygo popularized the genre of tropical house, blending traditional house music with steel drums and marimba sounds.
The Lexicon 224 digital reverb, released in 1978, became essential in electronic music studios for its lush spatial effects.
Donna Summer's 'I Feel Love,' produced by Giorgio Moroder in 1977, is considered one of the first electronic dance records.
The genre of folktronica combines folk music instruments and aesthetics with electronic production techniques.
Four Tet, born Kieran Hebden, is known for blending folk, jazz, and electronic music in his productions.
The genre of grime, which emerged in London in the early 2000s, blends elements of UK garage, jungle, and dancehall with electronic production.
Adam Beyer founded Drumcode Records in 1996, which became one of the most influential techno labels in the world.
Porter Robinson's 2014 album 'Worlds' departed from his earlier electro house sound and drew influence from anime and video game music.
The Akai S1000 sampler, released in 1988, was used extensively in early rave and jungle music production.
The genre of moombahton was created by DJ Dave Nada in 2009 by slowing down a Afrojack house track to 108 BPM.
The Elektron Analog Four is a four-voice analog synthesizer with a built-in sequencer, popular in live electronic performances.
Derrick Carter is a Chicago house DJ known for his eclectic sets blending disco, funk, and classic house.
The genre of future garage emerged around 2010 as a more atmospheric, downtempo descendant of UK garage.
The genre of dark techno uses distorted, industrial sounds and is associated with clubs like Berghain in Berlin.
The genre of melodic techno, popularized by artists like Tale Of Us and Anyma, combines driving techno rhythms with emotional melodies.
Chase & Status are a British duo who brought drum and bass to mainstream audiences with their 2008 album 'More Than Alot.'
The Korg MS-20 Mini, released in 2013, is a faithful miniaturized reproduction of the original 1978 MS-20 synthesizer.
Deadmau5 publicly feuded with several other DJs on social media, including a notable exchange with Skrillex about pressing play on stage.
The genre of techno-pop was pioneered by Yellow Magic Orchestra in Japan in the late 1970s.
The Native Instruments Massive synthesizer, released in 2007, became the go-to plugin for dubstep bass sound design.
The genre of hard techno typically operates at 140–160 BPM and features aggressive, distorted kicks.
Xfer Records Serum, released in 2014, became one of the most popular wavetable synthesizers in modern EDM production.
Kraftwerk's 'Trans-Europe Express' album, released in 1977, directly influenced Afrika Bambaataa's 'Planet Rock.'
The Paradise Club in Hersonissos, Crete, was one of the earliest Mediterranean venues to host international DJs.
The genre of acid techno combines the squelchy 303 sound of acid house with the harder, faster tempo of techno.
CamelPhat, a duo from Liverpool, won a Grammy nomination for their track 'Cola' in 2018.
The ARP Odyssey, first released in 1972, was one of the most popular duophonic synthesizers of the 1970s.
Avicii's 'Levels' used a vocal sample from Etta James's 'Something's Got a Hold on Me.'
The first Burning Man festival in 1986 was attended by only 20 people on Baker Beach in San Francisco.
Maceo Plex, born Eric Estornel, is known for blending techno, house, and electronica in his DJ sets and productions.
The Roland System-100M, released in 1979, was a modular synthesizer system that became popular in studios worldwide.
Black Coffee, born Nkosinathi Innocent Maphumulo, is a South African DJ who brought Afro house to global prominence.
The genre of complextro was coined by Porter Robinson and involves dense, layered electronic production with rapid sound changes.
The Arturia MatrixBrute, released in 2016, is a monophonic analog synthesizer with an extensive modulation matrix.
The genre of UK hard house was popular in British clubs in the late 1990s and early 2000s, featuring hard basslines at around 150 BPM.
Resident Advisor, launched in 2001, became the most important online publication for electronic music news and reviews.
The Arp 2600 was reissued by Korg in 2020, making the classic semi-modular synthesizer available to a new generation.
Richie Hawtin's 'Decks, EFX & 909' in 1999 was one of the first DJ mix albums to heavily incorporate effects processing.
The genre of glitch uses intentional audio artifacts, digital errors, and malfunctions as musical elements.
The genre of ambient house combines the repetitive beats of house music with the atmospheric textures of ambient music.
Jamie xx won a Mercury Prize nomination in 2015 for his solo album 'In Colour.'
The Ensoniq Mirage, released in 1984, was the first affordable sampler at under $1,700.
Venetian Snares is known for creating breakcore music in unusual time signatures, including 7/4 time.
The genre of tech trance blends the driving rhythms of techno with the melodic elements of trance.
Chris Liebing is a German techno DJ known for his technical skills and his weekly podcast 'Am/Fm.'
The Oberheim DMX drum machine, released in 1981, was one of the first programmable digital drum machines.
The genre of progressive trance features long, evolving compositions that gradually build over eight to ten minutes.
Bicep, a duo from Belfast, gained viral fame through their Tumblr blog 'Feel My Bicep' before becoming major electronic artists.
The Behringer Deepmind 12, released in 2016, offered 12-voice polyphonic analog synthesis at an unprecedented price.
The genre of dub techno was pioneered by Basic Channel in Berlin in the early 1990s, combining techno with dub delay effects.
David Guetta was born Pierre David Guetta and started DJing in clubs in Paris during the 1980s.
The genre of bounce music originated in New Orleans and features call-and-response vocals over electronic beats.
The TB-303's iconic squelchy acid sound is produced by its resonant low-pass filter and accent controls.
Aphex Twin won the first Electronic Music Award at the Classic Brit Awards in 2014.
The Buchla Easel, designed by Don Buchla in 1973, was a portable modular synthesizer used by avant-garde musicians.
The genre of downtempo, also called trip-hop, features slower BPM ranges and atmospheric, chilled-out soundscapes.
The Moog Sub 37, released in 2014, is a paraphonic analog synthesizer popular among modern synth players.
The annual Amsterdam Dance Event (ADE) is the world's largest club-based festival and conference for electronic music.
The Roland TR-8, released in 2014, digitally recreates the sounds of classic Roland drum machines in a modern format.
The genre of Balearic beat originated in Ibiza in the 1980s, blending house, pop, rock, and world music.
The Creamfields festival, first held in 1998 in Winchester, UK, is one of Europe's most established dance music festivals.
The genre of soulful house combines house music with R&B and gospel vocal styles and lush chord progressions.
Lee 'Scratch' Perry's dub production techniques in the 1970s directly influenced the use of effects in electronic music.
The Moderat project, a collaboration between Apparat and Modeselektor, blended IDM with melodic electronic pop.
The genre of acid house is defined by the squelchy, resonant sound of the Roland TB-303 bass synthesizer.
Robert Henke, co-creator of Ableton Live, is also an electronic musician performing under the name Monolake.
The Oberheim Matrix-12, released in 1985, was one of the most complex analog synthesizers ever produced.
Vangelis composed the 'Blade Runner' soundtrack in 1982 using a Yamaha CS-80 synthesizer, one of the most expensive synths ever built.
The genre of schranz is a hard, industrial form of techno that was popular in Germany in the early 2000s.
The Sequential Prophet Rev2, released in 2017, offers 16 voices of analog polyphonic synthesis at an accessible price.
The genre of trance is typically characterized by its tempo of 125–150 BPM and use of repeating melodic phrases.
Cirez D is the darker techno alias of Swedish producer Eric Prydz.
Disclosure, consisting of brothers Guy and Howard Lawrence, helped revive UK garage-influenced house music in the 2010s.
The genre of deconstructed club music emerged in the mid-2010s, pushing the boundaries of traditional dance music structures.
The Prodigy's Liam Howlett wrote and produced most of the band's music using an Akai sampler and Cubase software.
The genre of footwork originated in Chicago in the late 1990s, featuring rapid 160 BPM rhythms and intricate juke-influenced patterns.
The Roland Jupiter-8, released in 1981, is one of the most sought-after vintage analog synthesizers, known for its rich pads and leads.
Richie Hawtin's ENTER. events at Space Ibiza from 2012 to 2015 were known for combining technology, music, and sake.
The genre of darkwave blends elements of new wave and post-punk with cold, electronic production.
Knife Party released 'Internet Friends' in 2012, which became an anthem of the electro house and festival scene.
The Korg Wavestate, released in 2020, is a modern reinterpretation of the Korg Wavestation from 1990.
The genre of jersey club originated in Newark, New Jersey, featuring rapid-fire kick patterns at around 130–140 BPM.
Squarepusher, born Tom Jenkinson, is known for combining complex electronic production with virtuosic bass guitar playing.
The genre of tropical house was popularized by Thomas Jack and Kygo around 2014, featuring warm, breezy melodies.
Matthew Herbert has created electronic music using sounds from everyday objects, including food, war zones, and a human body.
The genre of hardwave combines elements of hardstyle, phonk, and wave music into an aggressive yet melodic hybrid.
The Roland TR-707, released in 1984, was one of the first MIDI-compatible drum machines.
The genre of Detroit techno is characterized by futuristic themes and a blend of electronic, funk, and soul influences.
The genre of minimal techno strips house and techno to their most essential elements, focusing on repetition and subtle variation.
Eric Prydz's 'Opus,' which runs for over nine minutes, became one of the most celebrated progressive house tracks of the 2010s.
Miss Kittin & The Hacker helped define the electroclash movement with their 2001 track 'Frank Sinatra.'
The Novation Peak, released in 2017, is an eight-voice polyphonic synthesizer combining analog filters with digital oscillators.
The genre of bleep techno, originating from Sheffield in the late 1980s, was characterized by sparse arrangements and deep sub-bass.
The genre of UK funky emerged in London around 2008, blending house music with African and Caribbean rhythmic patterns.
The Clavia Nord Modular, released in 1997, was one of the first virtual modular synthesizers.
The genre of synthpop, which emerged in the late 1970s, uses synthesizers as the dominant instrument and influenced later EDM.
Ólafur Arnalds blends classical strings with electronic beats and has won a BAFTA for his TV soundtrack work.
Frankie Knuckles was awarded a Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award posthumously in 2020.
The Korg Prologue, released in 2018, is a 16-voice analog synthesizer with a digital multi-engine.
Solomun, born Mladen Solomun, is a Bosnian-German DJ known for his residency at Pacha Ibiza.
The Novation Circuit, released in 2015, combined synth, drum machine, and sequencer in a portable groovebox.
David Guetta's collaboration 'When Love Takes Over' with Kelly Rowland reached number one in multiple countries in 2009.
Kraftwerk released 'Computer World' in 1981, which predicted the digital age with tracks about computers, software, and data.
The Ensoniq ESQ-1, released in 1986, was an early digital/analog hybrid synthesizer popular in electronic music.
The genre of EBM, or Electronic Body Music, emerged in Belgium in the early 1980s, blending industrial music with danceable rhythms.
The Roland System-8, released in 2016, uses ACB technology to digitally recreate classic Roland synthesizers.
The genre of UK bass is an umbrella term for bass-heavy electronic music originating from the United Kingdom.
The term 'drop' in EDM refers to the moment when the buildup reaches its peak and the bass and rhythm kick in.
The Waldorf Quantum, released in 2018, combines wavetable, granular, resonator, and particle synthesis in one instrument.
The first Awakenings festival was held in 1997 near Amsterdam and became one of the Netherlands' premier techno events.
The Novation Launchpad, released in 2009, became one of the most popular grid controllers for Ableton Live.
The genre of wonky, emerging around 2008, features off-kilter beats and detuned synthesizers.
DJ Shadow's 'Endtroducing.....' in 1996 was the first album created entirely from samples, according to Guinness World Records.
Modular synthesizer setups, known as 'modular rigs,' can cost tens of thousands of dollars and are customized by each musician.
Charlotte de Witte is a Belgian techno DJ who has become one of the biggest names in modern techno.
The E-mu SP-1200 sampler, released in 1987, has only 10 seconds of sample time but became iconic for its gritty sound.
The genre of melodic house and techno is characterized by long builds, dreamy pads, and driving rhythms.
The Pioneer DJM-900NXS2 mixer, released in 2016, is the industry-standard club mixer found in venues worldwide.
The genre of dancehall electronic blends Jamaican dancehall rhythms with electronic production.
The Korg Electribe series, first released in 1999, provided affordable groove machines for live performance and production.
The genre of big room house dominated festival main stages in the early 2010s with its massive drops and simple melodies.
The genre of drone music creates sustained, continuous tones and overlapping harmonics as its primary compositional technique.
Seth Troxler is an American DJ based in Berlin known for his marathon sets and contributions to deep house and techno.
Nils Frahm blends piano performance with modular synthesis and has become a leading figure in the neoclassical electronic genre.
The Yamaha Reface series, released in 2015, brought mini versions of classic Yamaha synthesizers to a new audience.
Fisher's hit track 'Losing It' in 2018 became one of the most played tech house tracks of the decade.
The Digidesign Pro Tools software, originally released in 1991, became the standard for professional audio recording and editing.
The Prodigy's 'The Fat of the Land' debuted at number one in 25 countries upon its release in 1997.
The Prodigy were formed in Braintree, Essex, in 1990 by Liam Howlett.
The genre of acid trance combines the TB-303 acid line with trance's melodic and rhythmic structures.
The Commodore Amiga computer was used by many electronic musicians in the late 1980s and early 1990s for its advanced audio capabilities.
The genre of laserwave is a subgenre of synthwave featuring more aggressive, space-themed production.
David Guetta was born in Paris on November 7, 1967, and began DJing at the age of 17.
Claptone is a mysterious house DJ who always performs wearing a golden Venetian-style mask.
The Teenage Engineering Pocket Operator series, launched in 2015, offers ultra-affordable portable synthesizers.
The genre of future rave was popularized by David Guetta and Morten, combining rave synths with modern EDM production.
Mixmag, founded in 1983, is one of the longest-running dance music publications in the world.
Tiësto was born Tijs Michiel Verwest on January 17, 1969, in Breda, Netherlands.
The Arturia MicroFreak, released in 2019, combines digital oscillators with an analog filter and a unique flat keyboard.
The first DJ controller, the Vestax VCI-100, was released in 2007 and helped popularize digital DJing.
The genre of post-dubstep emerged after 2010, moving away from aggressive bass drops toward more melodic and atmospheric sounds.
The Make Noise Shared System is a pre-configured Eurorack modular synthesizer designed for experimental electronic music.
Swedish House Mafia announced their breakup during a performance at Ultra Music Festival in 2012.
The genre of jersey bounce is a high-energy variant of jersey club that incorporates more melodic elements.
Derrick May's 'Strings of Life,' released in 1987, is considered one of the most important techno tracks ever made.
The Oxford English Dictionary added the term 'EDM' in 2018.
The Focusrite Scarlett audio interface series became the best-selling USB audio interfaces in the world.
Avicii was born Tim Bergling on September 8, 1989, in Stockholm, Sweden.
Daft Punk broke up on February 22, 2021, announced through a video titled 'Epilogue.'
Fabric nightclub in London was temporarily closed in 2016 due to drug-related incidents but was reopened after a widespread campaign.
Kraftwerk's co-founder Florian Schneider passed away on April 21, 2020.
Calvin Harris was born on January 17, 1984, in Dumfries, Scotland.
The first Tomorrowland festival was held in 2005 with only 9,000 attendees.
The first Ultra Music Festival was held on March 13, 1999, on the sands of Miami Beach.
Roni Size won the Mercury Music Prize in 1997 for 'New Forms,' a groundbreaking drum and bass album.
The first Electric Daisy Carnival was held in 1997 in Los Angeles.
The first Beatport digital music store launched in 2004, becoming the primary online marketplace for electronic music.
Skrillex was born on January 15, 1988, in Los Angeles, California.
Frankie Knuckles passed away on March 31, 2014, in Chicago.
Martin Garrix was born Martijn Gerard Garritsen on May 14, 1996, in Amstelveen, Netherlands.
Deadmau5 was born on January 5, 1981, in Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada.
Armin van Buuren was born on December 25, 1976, in Leiden, Netherlands.
Diplo was born Thomas Wesley Pentz on November 10, 1978, in Tupelo, Mississippi.
Avicii passed away on April 20, 2018, at the age of 28.
Marshmello was born Christopher Comstock on May 19, 1992.
Aphex Twin was born on August 18, 1971, in Limerick, Ireland.
Tomorrowland launched a digital festival called 'Tomorrowland Around the World' in 2020 due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
Kraftwerk was founded by Ralf Hütter and Florian Schneider in Düsseldorf, Germany, in 1970.
Daft Punk won Album of the Year at the 2014 Grammy Awards for 'Random Access Memories.'
The legendary DJ Larry Levan died on November 8, 1992, at the age of 38.
Marshmello performed at the Fortnite in-game concert on February 2, 2019.
The legendary DJ Andrew Weatherall passed away on February 17, 2020.
Goldie was born Clifford Joseph Price on September 19, 1965, in Wolverhampton, England.
The Prodigy's Keith Flint passed away on March 4, 2019.
Avicii's debut album 'True' was released on September 13, 2013.
Skrillex released his debut EP 'My Name Is Skrillex' as a free download on MySpace in 2010.
